name: PayNova
problem: Small merchants in tier-2/3 cities lack affordable payment infrastructure and real-time cash-flow visibility, leading to poor financial decisions and high loan rejection rates.
solution: PayNova offers an AI-powered payment gateway with built-in cash-flow forecasting, automated reconciliation, and a credit scoring module that uses transaction data to help merchants access working capital loans from partner NBFCs.
description: PayNova is a full-stack fintech platform built for India's 63 million small merchants. The platform processes ₹120 crore in monthly GMV and has a 94% merchant retention rate. Its proprietary credit engine has disbursed ₹45 crore in micro-loans with a sub-2% NPA. Backed by 3 fintech angels and shortlisted for RBI's regulatory sandbox.

name: WealthPath
problem: India has over 200 million salaried professionals with no access to personalized wealth management. Existing robo-advisors use generic portfolios that ignore individual risk psychology, tax brackets, and life goals.
solution: WealthPath uses behavioral finance algorithms and goal-based investing to create hyper-personalized mutual fund and ETF portfolios. It integrates with DigiLocker and GST portals to auto-populate financial profiles and rebalances portfolios dynamically based on life events.
description: WealthPath has onboarded 38,000 retail investors in 18 months with an average AUM of ₹2.2 lakh per user. Monthly SIP volume crossed ₹8 crore. The platform achieved SEBI RIA certification and has a 4.8-star rating on the App Store. Current NPS score is 72, indicating high user satisfaction.

name: InsureIQ
problem: India's insurance penetration is 4.2%, far below the global average of 7%, largely because claim settlement is opaque, slow, and agent-driven. Customers distrust insurers due to high claim rejection rates.
solution: InsureIQ is an insurtech platform that uses NLP and computer vision to automate claim processing, reducing settlement time from 30 days to 4 hours. It also provides an AI-underwriting engine licensed to 12 insurance companies and a D2C policy comparison platform.
description: InsureIQ processes 18,000 claims monthly with a 97.3% auto-approval rate. The B2B SaaS division generates ₹4.2 crore ARR from insurer clients. The D2C arm has 2.1 million registered users. The company was named India's Top Insurtech by IRDAI in 2023 and is in talks with two Southeast Asian insurers for international licensing.

name: LoanDost
problem: Rural India has 650 million adults, yet 73% are denied personal loans due to thin credit files. Banks rely solely on CIBIL scores, ignoring alternative data like utility payments, telecom usage, and agricultural income.
solution: LoanDost is an alternative credit scoring API that aggregates 47 data signals including Aadhaar-linked utility payments, UPI transaction patterns, and kisan credit card history to generate a Financial Health Score for unbanked borrowers.
description: In its 10-month pilot, LoanDost has scored 14,000 borrowers for partner microfinance institutions, achieving 88% approval accuracy vs 61% for CIBIL-only models. Three regional banks are in advanced API integration trials. The founding team includes ex-ICICI and ex-Experian data scientists.

name: TradeNest
problem: India's 35 million retail traders lose ₹2,200 crore annually due to emotional decision-making, lack of real-time risk alerts, and fragmented broker platforms that don't provide AI-driven insights.
solution: TradeNest is a broker-agnostic trading co-pilot that connects via broker APIs to provide real-time portfolio risk scores, sentiment-based entry/exit alerts derived from 800+ news sources, and an AI journaling feature that identifies behavioural trading patterns.
description: TradeNest has 92,000 active trader accounts with a 67% 90-day retention rate. Users who follow AI alerts show a statistically significant 18% improvement in returns vs control group. Revenue grew 3.4x in 12 months. SEBI-compliant and integrated with Zerodha, AngelOne, and Groww APIs.

name: MediScan AI
problem: India has only 1 radiologist per 100,000 people, causing diagnostic delays of 3-7 days for X-rays and CT scans. Misdiagnosis rates for TB and lung cancer in tier-2 hospitals exceed 34% due to fatigue and high workload.
solution: MediScan AI is a radiology AI platform that reads chest X-rays and CT scans in under 60 seconds with 96.4% sensitivity for TB, pneumonia, and lung cancer. It triages critical cases automatically and provides structured radiology reports in English and Hindi.
description: MediScan AI is deployed in 340 hospitals across 18 states. It processes 12,000 scans daily and has read over 2.4 million scans to date. The platform reduced average diagnostic turnaround from 5.2 days to 6 hours in partner hospitals. CE-marked, CDSCO approved, and selected for NITI Aayog's AI in Health initiative.

name: CareCircle
problem: India has 140 million elderly citizens, yet 68% live without formal care plans. Families are unable to monitor chronic conditions like diabetes and hypertension remotely, leading to preventable hospitalizations costing ₹15,000 per episode on average.
solution: CareCircle is a remote patient monitoring platform that uses IoT wearables, a medicine dispenser module, and an AI companion chatbot to track vitals and medication adherence. It sends real-time health alerts to family members and doctors, and auto-schedules teleconsults when vitals deteriorate.
description: CareCircle monitors 9,200 elderly patients across Mumbai, Bangalore, and Chennai. It has reduced unplanned hospital admissions by 41% in enrolled patients. The platform integrates with Apollo Telehealth and Practo. Monthly churn is under 3.5%. Recognized by WHO South-East Asia as a model for elderly care innovation.

name: NutriGen
problem: Standard diet plans ignore individual genetics, gut microbiome, and metabolic responses. 78% of people on generic diets relapse within 6 months. There is no affordable personalised nutrition solution in India.
solution: NutriGen combines at-home DNA testing, a gut microbiome analysis kit, and a continuous glucose monitor integration to generate a fully personalised weekly meal plan. An AI nutritionist chatbot explains recommendations and adjusts plans based on user feedback.
description: NutriGen has 1,800 paying subscribers with an average subscription value of ₹4,200/month. Early clinical trial with 200 participants showed a 31% improvement in HbA1c levels among pre-diabetic users over 90 days. Partnerships in progress with three corporate wellness programs.

name: SurgAI
problem: Surgical complications account for 50% of preventable deaths in Indian hospitals. Surgeons lack real-time intra-operative guidance systems affordable to public sector hospitals, which account for 70% of procedures.
solution: SurgAI's robotic surgical guidance system overlays AI-generated anatomical maps on the surgeon's view during laparoscopic procedures, flagging danger zones in real time. The system works with existing laparoscopic equipment via a clip-on sensor module, costing 90% less than standalone surgical robots.
description: SurgAI is deployed in 28 tertiary hospitals. It has assisted in 14,000 surgeries with a 0.3% complication rate vs the 3.1% sector average. Five government hospital empanelment contracts are active. The company holds 6 patents and has received CDSCO Class C device approval. Series B will fund US FDA submission and expansion into UAE and Saudi Arabia.

name: MindBloom
problem: India has 197 million people with mental health conditions, yet 83% receive no treatment. Corporate India loses ₹1.1 lakh crore annually to mental health-related productivity loss. Existing mental health apps lack clinical validation and employer integration.
solution: MindBloom is a clinically validated mental wellbeing platform that combines CBT-based self-help modules, AI mood tracking, peer support communities, and on-demand therapist video sessions. Employers get an anonymous population-level mental health dashboard and can trigger targeted interventions.
description: MindBloom is live with 44 enterprise clients including three Fortune 500 India subsidiaries. 82,000 employees are enrolled. Platform engagement is 4.1 sessions per week per user, 6x higher than industry average. Clinical outcomes show a 38% reduction in PHQ-9 depression scores over 8 weeks. Featured in ET Healthworld's Top 10 Digital Health Startups 2023.

name: SkillForge
problem: India's IT sector spends ₹8,200 crore annually on learning and development, yet 61% of employees report skills training is irrelevant to their actual work. LMS platforms are rigid, passive, and lack hands-on coding environments.
solution: SkillForge is an AI-driven enterprise upskilling platform that builds personalised learning paths based on an employee's current skills, role requirements, and performance data. It includes a live coding sandbox, AI mentor, real project simulations, and a skills-gap marketplace where companies can trade talent.
description: SkillForge serves 68 enterprise clients with 240,000 licensed users. Annual contract value averages ₹18 lakh per enterprise. The platform has a 91% course completion rate vs 15% industry average, driven by its adaptive microlearning engine. Shortlisted by LinkedIn Learning as a preferred partner in India. Net Revenue Retention is 128%.

name: CampusConnect
problem: India has 42,000 colleges, yet most lack a digital infrastructure for student-faculty collaboration, placement tracking, alumni networks, and event management. Colleges rely on WhatsApp groups and paper notices for critical communications.
solution: CampusConnect is an all-in-one campus management super-app offering digital classrooms, AI-powered placement preparation, alumni mentorship marketplace, college events, and a verified student social network. It integrates with existing ERP systems and provides a white-labelled app per institution.
description: CampusConnect is live across 380 colleges and 410,000 students. Placement success rate for enrolled students improved by 28%. The alumni marketplace has 14,000 mentors. Average revenue per college is ₹2.4 lakh annually. Raised a pre-seed round of ₹1.8 crore in 2023. Looking to expand to East Asia campuses.

name: GuruBot
problem: Over 15 million students in India prepare for JEE and NEET annually. Traditional coaching costs ₹2-4 lakh and is inaccessible for 60% of aspirants from rural areas. Existing apps offer pre-recorded content without personalised doubt resolution.
solution: GuruBot is a WhatsApp-native AI tutor that answers JEE/NEET doubts in text, voice, and image format within 10 seconds, provides step-by-step solutions in English and Hindi, and generates a personalised weak-topic study plan updated after each interaction.
description: GuruBot crossed 55,000 active monthly users in 8 months purely through word-of-mouth. Daily message volume exceeds 180,000 queries. Conversion from free to paid is 9.4%, above the edtech SaaS benchmark. The founding team includes an IIT Bombay alumnus and a former Vedantu curriculum head. Pilot integrations with two test-prep institutes underway.

name: LangLeap
problem: India's BPO and IT services sector needs 1.2 million English-proficient hires annually, yet 72% of engineering graduates fail communication assessments. Traditional English training is classroom-based, expensive, and slow.
solution: LangLeap is an AI-powered spoken English and business communication platform that uses voice AI for real-time pronunciation correction, scenario-based role-play (job interviews, client calls, presentations), and a personalised fluency roadmap. It offers a white-label API for HR teams to embed in onboarding workflows.
description: LangLeap has 1.6 million registered learners and 110,000 paid subscribers. B2B clients include Infosys, Mphasis, and 38 BPO companies. Average learner achieves CEFR B2 level in 90 days vs 9 months with conventional methods. Revenue grew 2.8x year-on-year. The platform recently expanded to Indonesia and Philippines.

name: VidyaVerse
problem: India's 1.5 million government school teachers lack digital teaching tools in local languages. Only 28% of classrooms are digitally equipped. Learning outcome gaps between private and government schools are widening at 3% per year.
solution: VidyaVerse delivers a tablet-based offline-first digital classroom platform in 14 Indian languages. It provides 3D animated curriculum aligned with NCERT, an AI-based teacher co-pilot for lesson planning, and a learning outcome tracker synced to DIKSHA portal.
description: VidyaVerse is deployed in 4,200 government schools across Rajasthan, UP, and Bihar under state government contracts. 680,000 students use the platform. Learning outcomes in enrolled schools improved by 34% in standardised assessments. Three new state tenders worth ₹48 crore are in final procurement stages.

name: SolarGrid
problem: India's 30 million SME manufacturers pay 22-28% of revenues on electricity. Rooftop solar adoption is under 3% among SMEs due to high upfront capital costs of ₹30-50 lakh and complex installation processes with no guaranteed returns.
solution: SolarGrid offers a zero-capex solar-as-a-service model for SME factories. It installs rooftop solar at no cost to the manufacturer and charges a per-unit electricity rate 30-40% below grid tariff over a 15-year PPA. Its proprietary IoT monitoring platform provides real-time energy analytics and predictive maintenance alerts.
description: SolarGrid has installed 48 MW across 260 SME clients in Maharashtra, Gujarat, and Karnataka. Monthly energy dispatched is 4.8 million kWh with 99.1% uptime SLA. Carbon credits generated are sold to 18 FMCG companies for Scope 3 offset. MNRE registered and empanelled with SIDBI green finance scheme.

name: HydroLoop
problem: Indian industry discharges 13.5 billion litres of wastewater daily, 74% of which is untreated. Water recycling plants are unaffordable for small manufacturers, and municipal water scarcity increases operational risk.
solution: HydroLoop manufactures modular decentralised water recycling units that plug into existing drainage lines. Treated water meets IS 10500 standards for industrial reuse. The unit is available on a lease model with pay-per-kilolitre pricing. An IoT dashboard tracks water quality, flow, and savings in real time.
description: HydroLoop has deployed 64 units across textile dyeing, food processing, and pharma manufacturers. Average client saves ₹8.4 lakh annually in water costs. The company is ISO 14001 certified and has a technology tie-up with IIT Madras's Water Research Centre. Shortlisted for Niti Aayog's Water Innovation Challenge 2023.

name: GreenFreight
problem: India's trucking sector emits 300 million tonnes of CO2 annually and is responsible for 45% of urban particulate pollution. Fleet owners resist EV transition due to high upfront cost, range anxiety, and poor charging infrastructure on highway routes.
solution: GreenFreight provides a route-optimised EV fleet management platform for last-mile and mid-mile logistics companies. It includes battery leasing to reduce capex, a proprietary charging corridor map with real-time availability, and automatic carbon credit generation and brokerage for fleet operators.
description: GreenFreight is running a pilot with 3 logistics companies in Delhi-NCR covering 80 EVs across 14 routes. Fuel savings average ₹18,000 per vehicle per month. Carbon credits generated are sold to e-commerce companies for ESG reporting. Pre-seed backed by a climate tech angel syndicate. Government EV subsidy advisor engaged.

name: WindCell
problem: India's renewable energy integration is limited by grid instability caused by the intermittent nature of wind and solar generation. DISCOM curtailment of renewable energy reached 18 billion kWh in FY23, costing ₹900 crore in stranded generation.
solution: WindCell develops co-located wind-solar hybrid plants with grid-scale battery storage (BESS) that deliver firm, schedulable renewable power. Its AI dispatch engine predicts generation 72 hours ahead and trades energy on the power exchange in real time, maximising returns for plant owners.
description: WindCell operates 280 MW of hybrid capacity in Gujarat and Rajasthan under long-term PPAs with DISCOMs and C&I buyers. EBITDA margin is 42%. It is the only Indian IPP with ISO 50001 energy management certification. The Series B will fund a 400 MW pipeline and a battery storage manufacturing JV with a South Korean partner.

name: CarbonLedger
problem: Indian companies face mandatory ESG reporting under SEBI's BRSR framework but lack standardised tools to measure, verify, and report Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions. Manual spreadsheet-based carbon accounting has 28% error rates and fails audits.
solution: CarbonLedger is an automated carbon accounting platform that integrates with ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, Tally), electricity bills, and supply chain data to auto-compute emissions. It uses blockchain for immutable audit trails and a built-in carbon offset marketplace to help companies achieve net-zero targets.
description: CarbonLedger serves 95 corporate clients including two Nifty 50 companies. Platform processes emissions data for 3.2 million tonnes of CO2 equivalent annually. BRSR report generation time reduced from 6 weeks to 4 hours. Partnership with BSE to provide data feeds for listed company sustainability rankings. GRI and TCFD reporting compliant.

name: CropMind
problem: India's 90 million smallholder farmers face ₹63,000 crore in annual crop losses due to pest attacks, nutrient deficiencies, and wrong sowing decisions. Agricultural advisory reaches less than 15% of farmers and is often generic and untimely.
solution: CropMind delivers hyper-local AI crop advisory via a WhatsApp chatbot and IVRS call line. It uses satellite multi-spectral imagery, IoT soil sensors, and historical crop yield data to provide field-level sowing, irrigation, fertiliser, and pest management advisories in 8 regional languages. It also aggregates input demand for bulk procurement discounts.
description: CropMind is used by 680,000 farmers across Telangana, Karnataka, and Maharashtra. Average yield improvement in enrolled farms is 22%. Input procurement aggregation has delivered 18% cost savings. Revenue generated from 3 agri-input companies as distribution partners. Recognised by NABARD and ICAR as a transformative precision agriculture platform.

name: FarmFinance
problem: Only 30% of Indian farmers have access to formal agricultural credit. Kisan Credit Card disbursement is hampered by paper documentation, land record disputes, and seasonal income patterns that traditional banks cannot assess.
solution: FarmFinance is an agri-lending platform that uses satellite-based crop health scores, land record API integration, and commodity price forecasting to generate a dynamic Farm Credit Score. It partners with cooperative banks and NBFCs to disburse crop and equipment loans digitally within 48 hours.
description: FarmFinance has originated ₹42 crore in loans to 8,200 farmers across 4 states. NPA is 1.4%, compared to 9% sector average, because its crop health model detects distress 60 days before harvest. Three more NBFC partnerships are in term-sheet stage. Pilot with Agri Sahkari Bank of Maharashtra underway for ₹100 crore co-lending arrangement.

name: ColdVault
problem: India wastes ₹92,000 crore in post-harvest food losses annually because cold chain infrastructure is fragmented, opaque, and inaccessible to smallholders. There is no single platform to track produce from farm to retailer.
solution: ColdVault operates a network of tech-enabled modular cold storage hubs near APMCs. IoT sensors track temperature, humidity, and ethylene gas levels for each produce lot. A marketplace platform lets farmers list storage inventory, retailers bid for supply, and logistics partners bid for transport, with full digital traceability.
description: ColdVault has 28 cold storage hubs across UP, Bihar, and West Bengal with 84,000 metric tonnes of capacity. 12,400 farmers are registered on the marketplace. Post-harvest loss among enrolled farmers fell 63% vs baseline. Big Basket and Reliance Retail are platform buyers. Three state government subsidy contracts for rural cold storage worth ₹22 crore are active.

name: PoultryIQ
problem: India's 800,000 poultry farmers lose ₹5,400 crore annually to mortality caused by disease outbreaks detected too late. Manual mortality tracking and feed management in large sheds is unreliable, leading to 8-12% flock loss rates.
solution: PoultryIQ installs an AI-powered computer vision system in poultry sheds to count birds in real time, detect early signs of disease (lethargy, abnormal gait), optimise automated feed dispensing, and alert farmers via SMS within 90 seconds of detecting anomalies.
description: PoultryIQ has deployed in 22 farms in Tamil Nadu with 1.4 million birds monitored. Mortality rates dropped from 9.2% to 2.8% in enrolled farms. Feed waste reduced by 14%. Three large integrator contracts in negotiation. IIT Madras Incubation Centre selected the team for its Agritech Cohort 2023.

name: AquaNova
problem: India's shrimp aquaculture industry loses ₹4,200 crore annually to disease and poor water quality management. Farmers rely on gut instinct for feeding and aeration decisions, resulting in 35% mortality in disease-prone seasons.
solution: AquaNova provides IoT water quality sensors (dissolved oxygen, pH, temperature, salinity) networked across shrimp ponds, with an AI feeding optimiser that dynamically adjusts feed dispenser schedules to reduce FCR by 18%. A direct-to-processor marketplace eliminates middlemen, improving farm-gate price by 22%.
description: AquaNova operates in 320 ponds across Andhra Pradesh and Odisha covering 480 acres. Enrolled farmers see disease incidence drop by 47% and yield increase by 19%. Processing company Avanti Feeds is a marketplace buyer. NFDB (National Fisheries Development Board) has co-funded 40 IoT installations under its aquaculture modernisation scheme.

name: NeuroChip
problem: AI inference on edge devices like cameras, drones, and industrial robots requires chips that are simultaneously powerful and energy-efficient. Existing chips from NVIDIA and Qualcomm cost 5-8x more than developing-market use cases can afford.
solution: NeuroChip has designed a custom neuromorphic AI inference chip (NCHIP-1) that delivers 40 TOPS at 2W power consumption, 70% cheaper than comparable alternatives. It is accompanied by an open SDK and pre-trained model library for vision, speech, and anomaly detection use cases.
description: NeuroChip has taped out NCHIP-1 at TSMC 7nm. Early access partners include a CCTV manufacturer (500,000 unit order), a drone OEM, and two Indian defence PSUs. IP licensed to a US semiconductor company for $2.1 million. Awarded India Semiconductor Mission grant of ₹12 crore. Team includes PhDs from IISc and ETH Zurich.

name: QuantumSafe
business_model: SaaS licensing to banks and defence
problem: Quantum computers capable of breaking RSA-2048 encryption are projected by 2030-2035. Banks, telecom companies, and government agencies storing sensitive data today face a 'harvest now, decrypt later' threat. Post-quantum cryptography migration is technically complex and has no turnkey solution.
solution: QuantumSafe provides a PQC (Post-Quantum Cryptography) migration platform that audits existing cryptographic infrastructure, generates a risk-prioritised migration roadmap, and provides NIST FIPS 203/204-compliant encryption libraries as a drop-in replacement SDK for Java, Python, and C++.
description: QuantumSafe is in paid PoC with two PSU banks, DRDO, and a major telecom operator. Pilots show 99.97% backward compatibility with existing systems. CERT-In has endorsed the platform's cryptographic audit methodology. The founding team includes ex-ISRO cryptographers and IIT-Delhi PhDs with 3 published papers on lattice-based cryptography.

name: BioFab
problem: Drug discovery takes 12-15 years and costs $2.6 billion on average. 90% of drug candidates fail in clinical trials because preclinical models poorly replicate human biology. India's pharma sector spends $1.8 billion annually on CRO services with limited access to cutting-edge platforms.
solution: BioFab has built a synthetic biology platform that engineers organ-on-chip tissue models replicating human liver, lung, and gut biology with 94% fidelity. Its AI-driven drug screening pipeline evaluates 10,000 compound-tissue interactions per day, compressing 18 months of pre-clinical work into 6 weeks.
description: BioFab has completed 52 drug discovery contracts with Indian pharma majors (Sun Pharma, Cipla, Lupin) and 8 global biotech firms. Three drug candidates discovered on the platform have entered Phase 1 trials. IP portfolio includes 14 patents. BIRAC-funded and recognised by DST as a Technology Business Incubator. Series B to fund US FDA GMP certification and open a Singapore operations centre.

name: HoloViz
problem: Industrial training for complex machinery (turbines, refineries, aircraft engines) involves physical risk, high cost, and geographic constraints. Traditional e-learning fails to replicate spatial, tactile, and procedural learning required for safe operations.
solution: HoloViz creates photorealistic holographic AR training simulations for industrial equipment. Using Microsoft HoloLens integration and a proprietary 3D asset pipeline, it deploys mixed reality training modules that are deployed on-premise. A trainee analytics dashboard tracks skill acquisition and compliance certification.
description: HoloViz is deployed in 3 ONGC refineries, 2 Adani power plants, and 1 HAL aircraft maintenance facility. Training time for complex procedures reduced by 55%. Compliance certification pass rates improved from 71% to 96%. Client NPS is 81. Three Fortune 500 industrial companies outside India are in evaluation. Government Skill India partnership in discussion.

name: RoboSort
problem: India's e-commerce sector ships 12 million parcels daily, yet warehouse sortation is 80% manual, error-prone, and expensive. Labour costs are rising 12% annually, and sorting accuracy is below 94%, causing significant return logistics costs.
solution: RoboSort deploys AI-powered autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) for warehouse sortation, picking, and packing. Its proprietary vision system achieves 99.7% sortation accuracy. The RaaS model requires zero capex from the client—robots are leased monthly with performance-based SLAs and maintained remotely via the cloud.
description: RoboSort has 340 AMRs deployed across 8 warehouses for clients including Flipkart, Delhivery, and two pharma distribution companies. Throughput per robot is 1,400 items/hour vs 320 for manual picking. Client EBITDA improvement averages 8 percentage points. A Series A round of $3M was raised in 2022. Series B preparation underway for FY25.

name: CloudStack Pro
problem: Indian mid-market companies (₹50-500 crore revenue) spend 28% more on cloud infrastructure than necessary because they lack FinOps expertise. Unoptimised cloud spend totals $4.2 billion annually across Indian enterprises.
solution: CloudStack Pro is a multi-cloud FinOps and governance platform that auto-detects idle, oversized, and unused cloud resources across AWS, Azure, and GCP. Its AI engine generates daily cost optimisation recommendations with one-click implementation, policy enforcement guardrails, and team-level spend accountability dashboards.
description: CloudStack Pro manages $380 million in cloud spend for 210 enterprise clients. Average client saves 34% on cloud bills within 90 days. Net Revenue Retention is 136%. It integrates with Jira, Slack, and ServiceNow for DevOps workflow compliance. Partnerships with AWS and Azure as a certified ISV partner unlocked co-sell pipeline worth $8 million.

name: HRMagic
problem: 65% of Indian SMEs still manage HR on Excel spreadsheets. Existing HRMS platforms (Darwinbox, Zoho) are over-engineered for companies with 20-200 employees, expensive, and require 3-6 month implementation cycles.
solution: HRMagic is a mobile-first HRMS platform targeting Indian SMEs with 20-250 employees. It provides attendance (face recognition + GPS), payroll, leave, PF/ESI compliance, and performance management in a single app that deploys in under 2 hours. An AI HR assistant answers policy queries and drafts offer letters.
description: HRMagic serves 2,800 SME clients covering 140,000 employees. Payroll accuracy is 99.94%. Average client onboarding time is 3.5 hours. Churn rate is 1.8% monthly. Revenue has grown 4.1x in 18 months. Integration with 14 banks for salary disbursement and automatic TDS computation. NASSCOM Emerge 50 company 2023.

name: LegalEase
problem: Indian companies collectively sign 2.4 billion contracts annually. Legal review costs ₹8,000-₹50,000 per contract via law firms. In-house legal teams of mid-market companies spend 60% of their time on contract drafting and review, leaving no time for strategic legal work.
solution: LegalEase is a contract intelligence platform that uses fine-tuned LLMs trained on 1.2 million Indian contracts to auto-draft, review, and negotiate NDAs, MSAs, vendor agreements, and employment contracts. It identifies non-standard clauses, flags risk, and suggests market-standard language with jurisdiction-aware advice for Indian law.
description: LegalEase has 480 corporate clients with 22,000 legal and business users. Average contract review time reduced from 4 days to 40 minutes. Legal cost savings per client average ₹28 lakh annually. Integrates with DocuSign, Adobe Sign, and major Indian e-stamp portals. Backed by 2 legal technology angels including a former Supreme Court lawyer.

name: SalesOrbit
problem: Indian B2B SaaS companies spend 38% of revenue on sales, yet 71% of reps miss quota. CRMs like Salesforce are complex, under-adopted, and don't help sales reps prioritise the right prospects at the right time.
solution: SalesOrbit is a sales intelligence and automation platform that integrates with existing CRMs to provide AI-generated deal health scores, automatic meeting summaries, next-best-action prompts, and a 'deal radar' that monitors intent signals from LinkedIn, news, and financial filings of target accounts.
description: SalesOrbit is used by 180 B2B SaaS and IT services companies in India. Sales reps using SalesOrbit close 2.3x more deals. Average deal cycle shortened by 28%. Integration with HubSpot, Zoho CRM, and Salesforce. Three large outsourced sales agencies have embedded the platform in their operations. Sequoia Scout recommended for India Surge accelerator.

name: SupplySync
problem: Manufacturing companies in India lose ₹1.8 lakh crore annually to supply chain disruptions. Existing ERP supply chain modules are rigid, built for stable environments, and unable to respond dynamically to multi-tier supplier disruptions, port congestion, or raw material price shocks.
solution: SupplySync is an AI-powered supply chain control tower that ingests data from ERP, logistics APIs, customs ports, weather feeds, and commodity exchanges to provide a real-time risk map of the entire supplier network. It auto-generates alternate sourcing recommendations, hedge triggers, and scenario playbooks.
description: SupplySync serves 68 enterprise manufacturers including Tata Steel, Bosch India, and two Japanese auto OEMs. Average client reduces supply disruption costs by 29%. The platform monitors 4.2 million SKUs and 18,000 tier-1/tier-2 suppliers in real time. NRR is 141%. Series B will fund expansion to ASEAN markets and an automotive vertical module.

name: StyleMart
problem: India's 70,000 independent fashion designers have no scalable platform to reach consumers beyond Instagram DMs and limited local clientele. Consumers want unique, artisan fashion but are unable to discover trustworthy indie designers.
solution: StyleMart is a curated D2C fashion marketplace for independent Indian designers. An AI style engine recommends designers based on body type, aesthetic preference, and occasion. Virtual try-on using AR reduces returns. Designers get inventory financing, logistics, and photoshoot services. StyleMart's private label fills gaps with trend-led basics.
description: StyleMart lists 4,200 independent designers with 280,000 SKUs. Monthly GMV is ₹22 crore growing at 8% month-on-month. Average order value is ₹3,800. Return rate is 14%, half the industry average, driven by AI-powered size recommendation. Virtual try-on adoption is 42% of sessions. Featured in Vogue India's Top Fashion Tech 2023.

name: GrocerHub
problem: India's kirana stores (12 million outlets) have inventory stockout rates of 18% and no demand forecasting tools. Distributors have poor visibility into last-mile demand. Online grocery penetration is 4%, leaving a massive market underserved.
solution: GrocerHub is a B2B2C platform that digitises kirana stores with a point-of-sale app, inventory management, and demand forecasting. Retailers order stock from GrocerHub's dark warehouses with same-day delivery. Consumers order groceries via the GrocerHub app fulfilled through the nearest digitised kirana store within 20 minutes.
description: GrocerHub has 34,000 digitised kirana stores across Delhi, Gurgaon, Noida, and Jaipur. Monthly GMV is ₹88 crore. Kirana partners earn 22% more revenue post-digitisation. Consumer app has 1.1 million monthly active users. Average delivery time is 17 minutes. Partnered with HUL, P&G, and ITC for direct distribution. Series B to fund 10 new city launches.

name: ArtisanNest
problem: India has 7 million registered artisans in handicrafts whose income averages ₹3,200/month, 40% below poverty line. They depend on middlemen who take 60-70% margin. Global demand for authentic Indian handicrafts is growing at 12% annually but artisans cannot access it.
solution: ArtisanNest is a global D2C marketplace connecting Indian artisans directly to buyers in 42 countries. It provides artisans with a smartphone-based product listing tool in regional languages, export documentation automation, GST-exempt small export facilitation, and USD payments via Payoneer.
description: ArtisanNest lists 8,400 artisans across Rajasthan, UP, and West Bengal. Monthly export GMV is ₹3.4 crore growing 14% month-on-month. Average artisan income increased 2.8x post-onboarding. US and Germany are top buyer markets. SEPC (Spices Export Promotion Council equivalent for handicrafts) has endorsed the platform. Government of Rajasthan is a co-promoter.

name: FreshRoute
business_model: Subscription box + a la carte orders
problem: India wastes 40% of fresh produce post-harvest due to broken supply chains. Urban consumers pay 80-120% premium at modern retail for produce that has spent 7-12 days in the supply chain. Farmers get only 23% of consumer rupee.
solution: FreshRoute is a farm-to-fork subscription platform that aggregates produce directly from 1,200 partner farmers at harvest and delivers personalised weekly vegetable and fruit boxes to urban subscribers within 24 hours of harvest. Route-optimised cold-chain vans serve 10km micro-zones. Subscription analytics help farmers plan future crops.
description: FreshRoute has 62,000 active subscribers across Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Pune. Average subscription value is ₹1,800/month. Farmer income improvement is 38% vs mandis. Produce freshness score (Brix test) is 94% vs 71% for modern retail. Monthly revenue growing at 6.2%. Series A to fund 4 new cities and proprietary cold chain infra.

name: PetParent
problem: India's pet care market is growing at 20% CAGR yet is highly fragmented. Pet owners lack access to veterinary consultation, quality nutrition, and emergency care outside metro cities. Existing platforms only serve tier-1 cities.
solution: PetParent is a full-stack pet care super-app combining a teleconsultation marketplace for vets, a personalised pet nutrition subscription (vet-formulated), a pet insurance module, pet-sitter/groomer discovery, and an emergency vet locator with 18-minute average response time.
description: PetParent has 48,000 pet owner users and 2,200 vet profiles. Monthly subscriptions are 8,400. App rating is 4.7 stars. Vet consultation NPS is 78. Partnered with Royal Canin, Hill's Pet Nutrition, and 3 pet insurance companies. Expanding to 12 tier-2 cities with offline vet clinic partnerships. First pet-focused startup to get ICAR (Animal Sciences) advisory board backing.

name: PixelRealm
problem: India has 568 million gamers but only 4% are paying users because game monetisation models are designed for Western markets with disposable income 8x higher. Indian game studios lack the IP, production quality, and retention mechanics to compete with global titles.
solution: PixelRealm is a mobile gaming studio building India-first games with mythological narratives, regional language support, and micro-payment monetisation optimised for ₹10-50 price points. Its proprietary Retention Engine uses ML-based content scheduling, live ops calendar automation, and dynamic difficulty adjustment.
description: PixelRealm's flagship title 'Mahayodha' has 14 million downloads and 2.8 million DAU in 18 months. ARPU is ₹42/month. The Retention Engine improved D30 retention from 8% to 22%. Three new titles in production with a co-development deal with a US publisher. Recognised by FICCI Frames as Best Mobile Game Studio India 2023.

name: MetaArena
problem: India has 400,000 mobile esports players competing in fragmented, unverified tournaments with high fraud, delayed prize payouts, and no structured ranking system. Brands cannot find reliable inventory to engage India's esports audience.
solution: MetaArena is a mobile esports tournament platform with skill-based matchmaking, blockchain-verified prize pools, a national ranking ladder, and a creator marketplace where streamers can host branded tournaments. Brands get real-time audience analytics and in-game ad inventory.
description: MetaArena has hosted 28,000 tournaments with 1.6 million unique players. Monthly tournament prize pool processed is ₹4.2 crore. Brand clients include Boat, Poco, and 3 gaming peripheral companies. Partnerships with BGMI, Freefire, and Call of Duty Mobile for official ranked events. User fraud rate is 0.4% vs industry 8%.

name: GenomeX
problem: India has the highest absolute burden of genetic disorders globally — 20 million children born with congenital conditions annually. Genetic testing costs ₹30,000-₹2 lakh and takes 8 weeks, making it inaccessible for 96% of affected families. Only 12% of genetic conditions have approved treatments.
solution: GenomeX has built India's first population-scale genomic sequencing and AI interpretation platform. Its next-generation sequencing panel covers 4,800 clinically actionable variants at a cost of ₹4,500. An AI diagnostic engine provides 98.1% accurate variant classification and auto-generates clinical reports in 72 hours.
description: GenomeX has performed 68,000 diagnostic tests at 240 partner hospitals and genetic counselling centres. It has India's largest variant database with 2.1 million Indian-ancestry data points, improving diagnostic yield by 31% over global references. Signed a clinical trial genomics deal with three global pharma companies worth $4.2 million. ICMR tie-up for national newborn screening programme.

name: PhageTech
problem: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) kills 700,000 people globally and 60,000 Indians annually. Antibiotic pipelines are drying up — only 4 new antibiotic classes have been approved in 40 years. Superbug infections in Indian ICUs have 47% mortality rates.
solution: PhageTech engineers bacteriophages (viruses that kill bacteria) as precision alternatives to antibiotics. Its phage library covers 38 AMR pathogen strains. A proprietary directed evolution platform designs new phage variants in 8 weeks. It supplies phage cocktail formulations to hospitals for compassionate use and is building an IND for Phase 1 clinical trials.
description: PhageTech has supplied phage treatments for 90 compassionate use cases at PGIMER and AIIMS Delhi with 74% clinical success rate. Published 3 peer-reviewed papers. Collaboration with Wellcome Trust India and DBT Biosciences cluster. BIRAC BIG grant recipient. Founding team includes PhDs from CSIR-IMTECH with 12 combined publications on phage therapy.

name: CellCraft
problem: India has 1.4 million new cancer cases annually. CAR-T cell therapy, which cures 60-80% of relapsed blood cancers, costs ₹3-5 crore per patient from Western manufacturers. Zero domestic manufacturing capability exists, leaving 99% of eligible Indian patients without access.
solution: CellCraft is India's first indigenous CAR-T cell therapy company. It has developed three CAR-T constructs (CD19, BCMA, CD22) manufactured in its GMP facility in Bangalore. Its end-to-end vein-to-vein process takes 18 days at a cost of ₹35 lakh, 90% cheaper than imported equivalents. A technology licensing deal with a US biotech is generating royalties.
description: CellCraft has treated 180 patients in ongoing Phase 2 trials with 71% complete remission in B-cell lymphoma. CDSCO approval application filed. ₹14 crore BIRAC Grand Challenge grant received. The manufacturing facility has capacity for 500 patients/year scalable to 2,000. Series B to build a second GMP facility and fund Phase 3 trial.
